info.name: RBAC – User Roles (/admin/rbac/users/{user_id}/role)
info.description:
E2E tests for the user role assignment API.

Routes covered:
  GET    /api/v1/admin/rbac/users/{user_id}/role
  PUT    /api/v1/admin/rbac/users/{user_id}/role
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/rbac/users/{user_id}/role

Folder execution order:
  1. setup            — register + Gmail OTP + verify-email + login
                        (captures ur_access_token — plain user, no RBAC permissions yet)
                        also registers a second target user (ur_target_user_id) to be assigned roles
  2. auth-guard       — 401 on all three routes with no Authorization header
  3. rbac-guard       — 403 on all three routes with valid JWT but no permissions
  4. owner-bootstrap  — POST /owner/bootstrap promotes the acting user to owner
                        (grants rbac:read + rbac:manage; RBAC middleware reads DB per request)
  5. happy-path       — GET (no role yet → 404) → PUT (assign role) → GET (assert role) → DELETE (remove role) → GET (assert gone → 404)
  6. conflict-guard   — 409 when attempting to modify own role or reassign owner
  7. not-found        — 404 when user_id resolves but has no role (GET/DELETE)
                        and 422 when role_id is unknown (PUT)
  8. validation       — 422/400/413 input-error cases for PUT

Guard order (handler):
  GET    /rbac/users/{user_id}/role   : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:read)   → handler
  PUT    /rbac/users/{user_id}/role   : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:manage) → handler
  DELETE /rbac/users/{user_id}/role   : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:manage) → handler

The admin router applies AllowContentType("application/json") before JWTAuth, so PUT
requests in auth-guard must carry Content-Type: application/json.

Environment variables required:
  bootstrap_secret   — must match BOOTSTRAP_SECRET in the running server's .env
  gmail_test_email   — Gmail address for OTP receipt
  gmail_access_token — OAuth2 token for Gmail API reads

Notes on what is NOT covered here (covered by unit/service tests):
  - DB / store errors → 500              — handler_test.go, service_test.go
  - UUID parse errors in service          — service_test.go
  - ErrLastOwnerRemoval DB trigger        — service_test.go

Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before each suite run.
Requires TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in .env.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so admin-route requests never collide on any shared bucket.

item.name: setup
item.description:
Registers two users:
  - Acting user (gmail_test_email): will be bootstrapped as owner and act as the caller for all RBAC operations.
  - Target user: a second account whose role will be assigned / removed by the acting user.

Both users are registered and verified. The acting user logs in and captures ur_access_token. The target user's ID is extracted from their login response and stored as ur_target_user_id.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API (async)...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in email body.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "ur_acting_code":', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] collection variable "ur_acting_code" is empty — Gmail fetch did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set and not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] Captured ur_access_token and ur_acting_user_id:', payload.sub);
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch for target user'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for target user verification email...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API for target user...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + targetEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found for target user.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode target email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in target email body.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "ur_target_code":', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] ur_target_code is empty — Gmail fetch for target user did not complete.');

item.name: POST /login (target user) → 200 (capture ur_target_user_id)
item.description: Target user email is now verified. Logs in and captures ur_target_user_id from the JWT sub claim.

exec: console.warn('[WARN] Could not extract target user ID from JWT sub.');

item.name: auth-guard
item.description: Verifies that the JWTAuth middleware rejects requests with no Authorization header before RBAC or handler logic is reached. Tests one request per HTTP method (GET, PUT, DELETE). PUT carries Content-Type: application/json so the AllowContentType router middleware passes before JWTAuth fires.

item.name: rbac-guard
item.description: Verifies that RBAC.Require rejects a valid JWT belonging to a plain user who has not yet been bootstrapped. Must return 403 before any handler logic is called.

item.name: owner-bootstrap
item.description: Promotes the acting user to owner by calling POST /owner/bootstrap with the correct bootstrap_secret. The owner role carries all permissions including rbac:read and rbac:manage. RBAC.Require reads the DB on every request — no re-login is needed; the existing ur_access_token is sufficient.

item.name: POST /owner/bootstrap → 201 (grant owner role to acting user)

exec: console.error('[ERROR] ur_access_token not set — setup/POST /login did not complete.');
console.error('[ERROR] bootstrap_secret not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
console.log('[INFO] Acting user is now owner — RBAC.Require(rbac:read) and RBAC.Require(rbac:manage) will pass on subsequent requests.');

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Full lifecycle for user role assignment authenticated as owner.

Execution order:
  1. GET  (target, no role yet)            → 404 user_role_not_found
  2. GET  /rbac/roles                      → capture ur_role_id (a non-owner, non-system role)
  3. PUT  (assign role to target)          → 200; assert UserRole shape
  4. GET  (target, role assigned)          → 200; assert same shape
  5. DELETE (remove role from target)      → 204
  6. GET  (target, role removed)           → 404 user_role_not_found

exec: if (!id) { console.error('[ERROR] ur_target_user_id not set — setup did not complete.'); }

item.name: GET /rbac/roles → 200 (capture a non-owner assignable role as ur_role_id)

exec: if (!roleID) { console.error('[ERROR] ur_role_id not set — GET /rbac/roles step did not complete.'); }
pm.test('granted_at is a parseable date', () => pm.expect(isNaN(new Date(body.granted_at).getTime())).to.be.false);
pm.test('granted_at is a parseable date', () => pm.expect(isNaN(new Date(body.granted_at).getTime())).to.be.false);

item.name: conflict-guard
item.description:
Verifies the self-modification guard (platformrbac.ErrCannotModifyOwnRole → 409 cannot_modify_own_role) for PUT and DELETE. The acting user is their own user_id — the service should reject this before any DB write.

Also verifies ErrCannotReassignOwner for PUT: attempting to PUT the owner role onto the target should return 409 cannot_reassign_owner.

exec: if (!actingID) { console.error('[ERROR] ur_acting_user_id not set — setup/login did not complete.'); }
if (!roleID) { console.error('[ERROR] ur_role_id not set — happy-path/GET /rbac/roles did not complete.'); }
if (!actingID) { console.error('[ERROR] ur_acting_user_id not set — setup/login did not complete.'); }

item.name: PUT /rbac/users/{target}/role — assign owner role → 409 cannot_reassign_owner
item.description: Attempts to assign the owner role to the target user via PUT. The store now rejects is_owner_role=true roles before the upsert → ErrCannotReassignOwner → 409.

exec: if (err || res.code !== 200) { console.error('[ERROR] Could not fetch roles:', err || res.code); return; }
if (!ownerRole) { console.warn('[WARN] No owner role found — test will not assert correctly.'); return; }
if (!ownerRoleID) { console.warn('[WARN] ur_owner_role_id not set — test skipped.'); return; }

item.name: not-found
item.description: Verifies 404 responses for user_role_not_found (GET and DELETE on a user with no role) and 422 for role_not_found (PUT with a role_id that is a valid UUID but does not exist in the DB).

item.name: DELETE /rbac/users/{target}/role — no active role → 404
item.description: The target user's role was already removed during happy-path. A second DELETE returns ErrUserRoleNotFound → 404.

exec: if (!id) { console.error('[ERROR] ur_target_user_id not set — setup did not complete.'); }

item.name: validation
item.description:
Body decode and field validation cases for PUT /rbac/users/{user_id}/role. All requests carry a valid JWT. Validation fires after RBAC.Require but before any service or DB call.

Rules from validators.go (validateAssignRole):
  role_id required (non-empty after trim)
  granted_reason required (non-empty after trim)

exec: pm.request.body.update({ mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ role_id: 'any', granted_reason: 'test', _pad: pad }) });

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest.
Set from gmail_test_email. Used by setup/register and login for the acting user.
Password for the acting user. Set in setup prerequest.
6-digit OTP fetched from Gmail during setup/register. Consumed by setup/verify-email.
JWT access_token from setup/login (acting user). Plain user before owner-bootstrap; owner after. Reused for all subsequent folders without re-login.
User ID of the acting user extracted from the JWT sub claim. Used for self-modify conflict tests.
Derived email for the target user (gmail+urtarget variant). Set in setup prerequest.
6-digit OTP fetched from Gmail during setup/register for the target user. Consumed by setup/verify-email (target).
User ID of the target user. Captured from the target user's JWT sub claim. Used as the {user_id} path parameter for all role-assignment requests.
ID of a non-owner, non-system assignable role captured from GET /rbac/roles. Used as role_id in PUT requests.
Name of the role captured alongside ur_role_id. Used for response shape assertions.
ID of the owner role. Fetched inline during conflict-guard/cannot_reassign_owner test.
